subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Hemophilia: When Clotting Fails
description: A 60-second lesson on Hemophilia: When Clotting Fails.

hook.text: Queen Victoria's bloodline carried a hidden gene that made her grandsons bleed unstoppably.

buildup.text: Hemophilia is a genetic disorder where the blood lacks essential clotting factors.

discovery.text: Without clotting factors, even minor injuries cause prolonged, dangerous bleeding.

twist.text: The disease spread through European royalty, possibly changing the course of the Russian Revolution.

climax.text: Modern treatment replaces missing clotting factors, allowing near-normal lives.

punchline.text: A queen's gene reshaped empires and advanced clotting science.

quiz.question: What causes hemophilia?
options: Missing or deficient blood clotting factors
